hypotheses.title: IRP1 [4Fe-4S] Cluster Occupancy as Feeding-Entrained Iron-Redox Chronostat
hypotheses.bridge: Feeding → dual iron supply + redox arms → IRP1 cluster occupancy → IRE-mRNA regulation
hypotheses.key_claim: IRP1 [4Fe-4S] cluster occupancy oscillates diurnally via feeding-entrained iron supply and redox convergence, providing a fast post-translational IRE-mRNA regulatory layer distinct from IRP2 transcriptional oscillation

hypotheses.title: Frataxin-Gated Fe-S Assembly Oscillation via Mitochondrial LIP in FTMT-Negative Tissues
hypotheses.bridge: Hepcidin-driven serum iron → mitochondrial LIP (unbuffered in FTMT-absent liver) → frataxin iron donation → Fe-S assembly rate
hypotheses.key_claim: Liver mitochondria lack FTMT, creating an unbuffered mitochondrial LIP that amplifies diurnal plasma iron oscillation into frataxin-dependent Fe-S assembly rate oscillation

hypotheses.title: CISD2 [2Fe-2S] as Redox-Gated ER-Mitochondrial Calcium Timer (Forward Direction Only)
hypotheses.bridge: NAD+/NADH oscillation → CISD2 [2Fe-2S] redox cycling → MAM conformation → ER-mito Ca2+ transfer
hypotheses.key_claim: CISD2's stable-but-redox-sensitive [2Fe-2S] cluster acts as a molecular redox sensor at MAMs, converting circadian NAD+/NADH oscillations into oscillating ER-mitochondrial calcium transfer

hypotheses.title: Cytoplasmic Prx1/2 H2O2 Oscillation as Non-Transcriptional Timer for Cytoplasmic Fe-S Proteins
hypotheses.bridge: Prx2 circadian hyperoxidation → cytoplasmic H2O2 transient → IRP1/ABCE1/XPD [4Fe-4S] cluster damage
hypotheses.key_claim: Circadian Prx1/2 hyperoxidation cycles generate transient cytoplasmic H2O2 pulses that selectively damage labile [4Fe-4S] clusters on cytoplasmic Fe-S proteins

hypotheses.title: NADPH→FDXR→FDX2 Electron Supply Chain as Circadian Fe-S Assembly Gatekeeper
hypotheses.bridge: NADPH circadian oscillation → FDXR → FDX2 reduction + FDX2 cluster stability → FDX2:FXN stoichiometric balance → Fe-S assembly rate
hypotheses.key_claim: The FDX2:FXN stoichiometric constraint (Lill 2025) makes Fe-S assembly exquisitely sensitive to circadian FDX2 oscillation via stoichiometric deviation

hypotheses.title: CIA Pathway as LIP/ROS-Responsive Circadian Gate for Cytoplasmic Fe-S Proteome
hypotheses.bridge: Diurnal LIP + ROS oscillation → CIAO3 interaction dynamics → CIA efficiency → coordinated maturation of ~20 cytoplasmic Fe-S proteins
hypotheses.key_claim: CIAO3's documented sensitivity to LIP and ROS creates a previously unrecognized circadian gatekeeper for the entire cytoplasmic Fe-S proteome

hypotheses.title: Conserved Fe-S Requirement in Clock Neurons — Drosophila to Mammalian SCN
hypotheses.bridge: Drosophila Fe-S gene RNAi → circadian disruption (Mandilaras 2012) predicts mammalian SCN NFS1 conditional KO → arrhythmicity
hypotheses.key_claim: The 14-year-old Drosophila finding predicts a conserved mammalian phenotype: conditional NFS1 knockout in SCN neurons will abolish circadian behavioral rhythms

diversity_classes.H2.2: Substrate supply bottleneck (iron → frataxin, mitochondrial compartment)
diversity_classes.H2.3: ER-mitochondria Ca2+ signaling (CISD2 redox sensor at MAMs)
diversity_classes.H2.4: Non-transcriptional redox timer (Prx2 → H2O2 → cytoplasmic Fe-S)
diversity_classes.H2.5: Electron supply + stoichiometric constraint (NADPH → FDX2, Lill 2025)
diversity_classes.H2.6: Cytoplasmic maturation pathway (CIA → CIAO3 LIP/ROS → Fe-S proteome)
diversity_classes.H2.7: Reverse direction / conservation (Fe-S → clock in neurons)

H2_CISD2: cluster half-life refined via pH stability data
H3_Prx: Prx3 resistance acknowledged - redirected to cytoplasmic Prx1/2
cytoplasmic H2O2 transients during Prx2 hyperoxidation
cytoplasmic Fe-S proteins (IRP1, ABCE1, XPD) targeted instead
H4_IRP1: feeding → postprandial iron + NADH dual mechanism specified
IRP2 KO mouse separation test designed
~2-3 fold IRP1 vs 10-fold IRP2 magnitude quantified
H8_Frataxin: no published diurnal LIP hepatocyte data - acknowledged as gap
mitoferrin circadian unknown - acknowledged as gap and testable prediction
FA carrier power analysis: n=20/group, 4 timepoints, power ~0.7
